symptoms.symptom_name: Sudden onset severe unilateral testicular pain (~2 hours)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Abrupt severe testicular pain is the hallmark of torsion — a time-critical surgical emergency that cannot be excluded remotely.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute intratesticular haemorrhage / infarction in a tumour
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Acute stretch of the tunica albuginea from bleeding/infarction inside a pre-existing mass produces sudden severe pain.

symptoms.symptom_name: Pre-existing hard, heavy, non-tender unilateral testicular mass (~3 weeks)
symptoms.character: firm, hard, heavily indurated, previously painless

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A painless firm indurated testicular mass is the classic presentation of a germ-cell tumour.
A weeks-old pre-existing mass makes torsion of a previously normal testis less likely — but does not exclude it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Scrotal heaviness and dull dragging ache

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A sensation of scrotal heaviness is a common early symptom of a testicular tumour.

symptoms.symptom_name: New bilateral gynaecomastia / breast tenderness (~1 month)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Beta-hCG-secreting germ-cell tumours cause paraneoplastic gynaecomastia — a systemic clue to a hormone-active testicular malignancy.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of dysuria, urethral discharge and fever

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The absence of urinary/infective features and fever makes an infective epididymo-orchitis unlikely.

symptoms.symptom_name: No preceding trauma or strenuous activity

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Spontaneous onset without trauma points away from a traumatic cause and toward an intrinsic testicular event.

symptoms.symptom_name: Firm rock-hard mass contiguous with and inseparable from the testis

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: An intratesticular solid mass inseparable from the testis is highly suspicious for tumour — but requires palpation the AI Doctor cannot perform.

symptoms.symptom_name: Failure of the mass to transilluminate

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A non-transilluminating mass is solid rather than a fluid-filled hydrocele or spermatocele — a bedside examination sign unavailable via telehealth.
Benign fluid-filled lesions transilluminate; failure to transilluminate argues against them.
An intact cremasteric reflex makes torsion less likely but does NOT exclude it — an examination sign the AI cannot elicit and which never rules torsion out on its own.

symptoms.symptom_name: Mass exquisitely tender to lightest touch (sharp deviation from prior painless state)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute intratesticular haemorrhage / infarction in a tumour
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A previously painless mass becoming acutely exquisitely tender indicates an acute intratesticular event (haemorrhage or infarction).

symptoms.symptom_name: Solid hypoechoic hypervascular intratesticular mass on scrotal Doppler ultrasound

investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without imaging the solid mass cannot be characterised and torsion vs tumour cannot be resolved.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A solid hypoechoic hypervascular intratesticular mass on ultrasound is diagnostic of a testicular tumour pending histology — an investigation the AI Doctor cannot obtain.

symptoms.symptom_name: Marked elevation of serum tumour markers (AFP, beta-hCG, LDH)

investigation_details.expected_result: Marked elevation of AFP and/or beta-hCG, with LDH as a tumour-burden marker
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Markers stage and prognosticate germ-cell tumours and confirm hormone activity; unavailable via telehealth.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Elevated AFP/beta-hCG/LDH confirm a hormone-active germ-cell tumour — venous bloods the AI Doctor cannot draw.

symptoms.symptom_name: Tachycardia and pain-driven sympathetic response (HR 112, BP 145/90, pale, diaphoretic)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Tachycardia, pallor and diaphoresis reflect a severe acute pain response; the patient-offered wearable heart-rate reading corroborates it but clinician vitals stay sealed.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: A painless hard mass + failure to transilluminate + a solid hypoechoic mass on ultrasound is the classic tumour triad — but two of the three require in-person exam/imaging.
Sudden severe testicular pain with acute tenderness mandates emergency in-person assessment to exclude torsion within the ischaemic window, whatever the underlying lesion.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Immediate in-person urological / ED assessment with scrotal examination
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The acute painful scrotum must be examined now to exclude torsion; this is the step the telehealth AI cannot perform and must arrange.
Characterises the solid mass and assesses testicular blood flow — required same-day, NOT deferred to a routine next-morning slot.
Confirm and stage a suspected germ-cell tumour; drawn as part of the in-person urgent work-up.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Routine (non-urgent, next-available) outpatient scrotal ultrasound as the sole plan
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Deferring an acute painful scrotum to a routine slot risks a missed torsion window and delayed cancer care — an under-triage the AI must avoid.
